symptoms.symptom_name: Dysuria + frequency/urgency + suprapubic discomfort + cloudy urine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dysuria with frequency/urgency and suprapubic discomfort in a woman has a high probability of cystitis, allowing empirical treatment.
Absence of fever/loin pain/systemic features argues strongly against pyelonephritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vaginal discharge; non-pregnant/no complicating factors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of vaginal discharge/irritation lowers the probability of vaginitis/STI as the cause of dysuria.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Clinical diagnosis is sufficient for typical uncomplicated cystitis; treat empirically.
Culture if recurrent/failed/complicated; pregnancy test to confirm status and guide antibiotic choice.
